old_value: {"id":"mg-zoy","title":"Prefer SourceCLI over stale JSONL when bd is available","description":"mg walks up the directory tree looking for .beads/issues.jsonl and reads it if found. But beads v0.56+ stopped writing JSONL, so old files persist and contain stale data. This causes mg to show wrong issues instead of falling back to bd list --json (SourceCLI mode). Fix: when both JSONL and bd are available, check JSONL age or always prefer SourceCLI on beads 0.56+. Affected files: internal/data/source.go (resolveSource), internal/data/loader.go.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"matthew.wright@roobee.co","created_at":"2026-02-25T16:40:16Z","created_by":"matt-wright86","updated_at":"2026-02-25T16:40:16Z"}

new_value: fixed in 023866b — CLI now preferred over stale JSONL

old_value: {"id":"mg-8be","title":"Parse parked rig error from gt sling and show specific toast","description":"Gas Town 01fdc51 added IsRigParked() checks to gt sling and gt convoy. When a rig is parked, sling fails with an error. mg should parse this error from stderr and show a specific toast ('Rig is parked — run gt rig unpark \u003cname\u003e to resume') instead of a generic failure message. Optionally add a parked indicator to the agent roster.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"matthew.wright@roobee.co","created_at":"2026-02-25T16:21:47Z","created_by":"Matthew Wright","updated_at":"2026-02-25T16:21:47Z"}

new_value: implemented in 885cd93 — typed RigParkedError + warning toast with unpark command

old_value: {"id":"mg-aun","title":"Review tmux socket handling for multi-town compatibility","description":"Gas Town 209b47b and 8d5264f migrated all tmux callsites to a socket-aware API that parses the socket path from the TMUX env var instead of hardcoding the town name. Our internal/tmux/ and internal/agent/ packages may need similar updates to work correctly in multi-town setups.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"matthew.wright@roobee.co","created_at":"2026-02-25T16:21:47Z","created_by":"Matthew Wright","updated_at":"2026-02-25T16:21:47Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"## Review Findings (2026-02-25)

**Result: No code changes needed.** mg's tmux handling is already multi-town compatible.

### Analysis

1. **TMUX env var auto-routing**: When mg runs inside a tmux session, the TMUX env var (format: /socket/path,pid,session_idx) is inherited by all child processes. Bare tmux commands automatically connect to the correct server without -S flags.

2. **Guard: InTmux()**: All tmux features in internal/agent/tmux.go are gated by InTmux() which checks TMUX != ''. mg never targets a remote/different tmux server.

3. **Upstream context**: The socket-aware commits (209b47b, 8d5264f, 37441ee) are for Gas Town's daemon/mayor which manage tmux sessions from OUTSIDE tmux. mg only splits panes within its current session — fundamentally different use case.

4. **list-panes -a scoping**: The -a flag lists all panes on the current SERVER (scoped by TMUX env var), not all servers on the machine. Multi-town setups with different sockets are naturally isolated.

### Files Reviewed
- internal/agent/tmux.go (6 bare tmux exec.Command calls)
- internal/agent/launch.go (no tmux interaction)
- internal/gastown/sling.go (HandoffInTmux — 1 bare tmux call)
- ~/go/pkg/mod/.../gastown@v0.7.0/internal/tmux/tmux.go (upstream reference)

### Edge Case (Low Risk)
If two mg instances run on the same tmux server (different sessions), ListAgentWindows() returns both instances' panes. But @mg_agent tags use beads issue IDs which are prefix-scoped, so no collision."}
old_value: {"id":"mg-aun","title":"Review tmux socket handling for multi-town compatibility","description":"Gas Town 209b47b and 8d5264f migrated all tmux callsites to a socket-aware API that parses the socket path from the TMUX env var instead of hardcoding the town name. Our internal/tmux/ and internal/agent/ packages may need similar updates to work correctly in multi-town setups.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"matthew.wright@roobee.co","created_at":"2026-02-25T16:21:47Z","created_by":"Matthew Wright","updated_at":"2026-02-25T17:32:37Z"}

new_value: Review complete: no code changes needed. mg tmux handling is multi-town compatible via TMUX env var inheritance.

{"id":"mg-2wm","title":"Surface AgentInfo, AgentAlias, FirstSubject in Gas Town panel","description":"Three new AgentRuntime fields landed upstream (Gas Town, pre-v0.8.0 on main): AgentInfo (runtime/model e.g. 'claude/opus'), AgentAlias (short name), FirstSubject (first unread mail subject). The struct fields are already in internal/gastown/status.go (lines 31-33) but not rendered in the Gas Town panel view. Should show AgentInfo as a subtle badge next to agent name, AgentAlias as display name when available, and FirstSubject as a mail indicator.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"matthew.wright@roobee.co","created_at":"2026-02-26T21:10:58Z","created_by":"matt-wright86","updated_at":"2026-02-26T21:10:58Z"}

{"id":"mg-85b","title":"Re-add Running field to AgentRuntime for zombie detection","description":"The Running bool field was removed from mg's AgentRuntime struct when we believed it was removed upstream in v0.8.0. The 2026-02-25 upstream check confirmed it is still present in Gas Town source. Re-adding it would improve zombie detection in internal/gastown/problems.go — an agent with State='working' but Running=false is a strong zombie signal. Field exists at status.go:21 but is silently discarded during JSON unmarshal since we removed it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"matthew.wright@roobee.co","created_at":"2026-02-26T21:11:02Z","created_by":"matt-wright86","updated_at":"2026-02-26T21:11:02Z"}

new_value: Running field already present in status.go:21, used in problems.go:51 for zombie detection
conditional-blocks already in DefaultBlockingTypes at issue.go:60
AgentAlias display implemented; AgentInfo and FirstSubject were already surfaced

old_value: {"id":"mg-ex9","title":"Plan SourceCLI-only future: deprecate SourceJSONL mode","description":"Gas Town PR #2051 (open) removes all JSONL fallback paths from gastown. Beads main removed JSONL entirely in v0.56. Our SourceJSONL mode in internal/data/source.go and internal/data/loader.go still reads issues.jsonl directly, but this format is dead upstream. Should plan deprecation: make SourceCLI the default and only mode, remove JSONL parsing code, simplify the Source struct. Not urgent since SourceCLI already works and is preferred, but the JSONL code is dead weight.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"matthew.wright@roobee.co","created_at":"2026-02-26T21:11:10Z","created_by":"matt-wright86","updated_at":"2026-02-26T21:11:10Z"}

new_value: SourceCLI now preferred over SourceJSONL; JSONL kept as legacy fallback

old_value: {"id":"mg-nse","title":"Surface gt vitals health dashboard in Gas Town panel","description":"New gt vitals command (462e526) provides unified health dashboard: Dolt server status, per-rig issue statistics via SQL, and backup state.
